content:
# Disagreement Log — Numbers 15 (NUM_015)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA

---

### Verse 2
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "When you come unto the land of your dwellings which I am giving to you" |
| **LOGOS** | "When you come into the land of your dwelling places which I am giving to you" |
| **PNEUMA** | "When you enter the land where you will settle, which I am giving to you" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA adopts LOGOS: "When you come into the land of your dwelling places, which I am giving to you." PNEUMA's "enter...where you will settle" is a dynamic paraphrase; the Hebrew *erets moshvoteikhem* is literally "land of your dwelling places." EMET's "unto" is archaic. |

### Verse 3
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "and you make a fire-offering to YHWH, a burnt-offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow or as a freewill-offering or in your appointed feasts, to make a pleasing aroma to YHWH, from the herd or from the flock" |
| **LOGOS** | "and you make an offering by fire to YHWH -- a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow, or as a freewill offering, or in your appointed feasts, to make a pleasing aroma to YHWH, from the herd or from the flock" |
| **PNEUMA** | "and you prepare a fire offering to YHWH -- a burnt offering or a sacrifice, whether fulfilling a special vow, or as a freewill offering, or at your appointed festivals -- to produce a soothing aroma for YHWH, from the herd or from the flock" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA adopts a blend favoring LOGOS/EMET: "and you make a fire offering to YHWH -- a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow, or as a freewill offering, or at your appointed feasts, to make a pleasing aroma to YHWH, from the herd or from the flock." PNEUMA's "prepare," "special vow," "festivals," and "soothing aroma" diverge from the Hebrew. "Pleasing aroma" is the standard rendering of *re'akh nikhoakh*. "Feasts" rather than "festivals" for *mo'adim*. |

### Verse 4
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "and shall bring near the one bringing near his offering to YHWH, a grain-offering of fine flour, a tenth, mixed with a fourth of the hin of oil" |
| **LOGOS** | "then the one who brings his offering shall present to YHWH a grain offering of one-tenth of an ephah of fine flour, mixed with one-fourth of a hin of oil" |
| **PNEUMA** | "then the one presenting his offering to YHWH shall bring as a grain offering one-tenth of an ephah of fine flour, mixed with one-quarter of a hin of oil" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA follows LOGOS with a small adjustment: "then the one who brings near his offering to YHWH shall present a grain offering of one-tenth of an ephah of fine flour, mixed with one-fourth of a hin of oil." EMET's cognate doubling ("shall bring near the one bringing near") is too literal for the consensus. "One-fourth" preferred over "one-quarter" for consistency. |

### Verse 6
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "Or for the ram, you shall make a grain-offering of fine flour, two-tenths mixed with oil, a third of the hin" |
| **LOGOS** | "Or for a ram, you shall prepare a grain offering of two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour, mixed with one-third of a hin of oil" |
| **PNEUMA** | "Or for a ram, you shall prepare as a grain offering two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour, mixed with one-third of a hin of oil" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA follows LOGOS/PNEUMA: "Or for a ram, you shall prepare a grain offering of two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour, mixed with one-third of a hin of oil." EMET's "the ram" (with article) and "make" differ. LOGOS and PNEUMA are nearly identical here. |

### Verse 8
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "And when you make a son of the herd as a burnt-offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow or peace-offerings to YHWH" |
| **LOGOS** | "And when you prepare a bull as a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow, or as a peace offering to YHWH" |
| **PNEUMA** | "And when you prepare a young bull as a burnt offering or as a sacrifice, whether fulfilling a special vow or as a peace offering to YHWH" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA: "And when you prepare a young bull as a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow, or as peace offerings to YHWH." EMET's "son of the herd" is the literal Hebrew idiom (*ben baqar*) but "young bull" is clearer English. PNEUMA's "special vow" adds an interpretive gloss. The Hebrew *shelamim* is plural ("peace offerings"), preserved here. |

### Verse 11
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "Thus shall be done for the one ox, or for the one ram, or for the one of the flock among the young rams or among the goats" |
| **LOGOS** | "Thus shall it be done for each bull, or for each ram, or for each of the male lambs or of the goats" |
| **PNEUMA** | "This is what shall be done for each bull, or for each ram, or for each young sheep or goat" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA: "Thus shall it be done for each bull, or for each ram, or for each of the flock, whether of the lambs or of the goats." EMET's "the one ox" is overly literal. PNEUMA's "young sheep or goat" simplifies the Hebrew. The Hebrew *seh ba-khvasim o va-izzim* ("one of the flock, among the lambs or among the goats") is best captured with the compromise wording. |

### Verse 13
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "Every native-born shall do thus these, to bring near a fire-offering of a pleasing aroma to YHWH" |
| **LOGOS** | "Every native shall do these things in this manner, to present an offering by fire, a pleasing aroma to YHWH" |
| **PNEUMA** | "Every native-born shall do these things in this way when presenting a fire offering of soothing aroma to YHWH" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA: "Every native-born shall do these things in this manner, to present a fire offering of a pleasing aroma to YHWH." "Native-born" preferred over "native" (EMET/PNEUMA vs. LOGOS). "Pleasing aroma" rather than "soothing aroma" (PNEUMA). |

### Verse 14
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "And when sojourns with you a sojourner, or whoever is in your midst for your generations, and he makes a fire-offering of a pleasing aroma to YHWH -- as you do, thus he shall do" |
| **LOGOS** | "And when a sojourner sojourns with you, or whoever is among you throughout your generations, and he makes an offering by fire, a pleasing aroma to YHWH -- as you do, so shall he do" |
| **PNEUMA** | "And when a foreigner resides among you, or whoever is in your midst throughout your generations, and he prepares a fire offering of soothing aroma to YHWH -- just as you do, so shall he do" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA: "And when a sojourner sojourns with you, or whoever is in your midst throughout your generations, and he makes a fire offering of a pleasing aroma to YHWH -- as you do, so shall he do." PNEUMA's "foreigner resides" loses the cognate construction *ger...yagur* ("sojourner sojourns"); SOPHIA preserves it with EMET/LOGOS. |

### Verse 15
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "The assembly -- one statute for you and for the sojourner the sojourning; a statute of perpetuity for your generations: as you, the sojourner shall be before YHWH" |
| **LOGOS** | "As for the assembly, there shall be one statute for you and for the sojourner who sojourns with you, a perpetual statute throughout your generations. As you are, so shall the sojourner be before YHWH" |
| **PNEUMA** | "As for the assembly, there shall be one statute for you and for the foreigner who resides among you -- an eternal statute throughout your generations. As you are, so shall the foreigner be before YHWH" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA uses "foreigner" instead of "sojourner" and "eternal" instead of "perpetual." EMET's phrasing is too compressed. |

### Verse 16
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "One law and one judgment shall be for you and for the sojourner the sojourning with you" |
| **LOGOS** | "One law and one ordinance shall be for you and for the sojourner who sojourns with you" |
| **PNEUMA** | "One law and one standard of justice shall apply to you and to the foreigner who resides among you" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA follows LOGOS: "One law and one ordinance shall be for you and for the sojourner who sojourns with you." PNEUMA's "standard of justice" is interpretive for *mishpat*; "ordinance" is the standard formal rendering. EMET's "judgment" is acceptable but "ordinance" is more precise for this legal context. |

### Verse 18
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "Speak unto the Sons of Israel, and say unto them: In your coming unto the land which I am bringing you there" |
| **LOGOS** | "Speak to the Sons of Israel and say to them: When you come into the land to which I am bringing you" |
| **PNEUMA** | "Speak to the Sons of Israel and say to them: When you come into the land to which I am bringing you" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA follows LOGOS/PNEUMA (identical): "Speak to the Sons of Israel and say to them: When you come into the land to which I am bringing you." EMET's "In your coming...there" is overly literal for the infinitive construct with directional *shammah*. |

### Verse 19
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "and it shall be, in your eating from the bread of the land, you shall raise a contribution to YHWH" |
| **LOGOS** | "then it shall be that when you eat of the bread of the land, you shall present a contribution to YHWH" |
| **PNEUMA** | "and when you eat from the bread of the land, you shall set aside a contribution for YHWH" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA: "then it shall be, when you eat from the bread of the land, you shall present a contribution to YHWH." EMET's "raise" is literal for *tarimu* but "present" (LOGOS) is clearer English. PNEUMA's "set aside" departs from the Hebrew *rum* ("to raise/lift"). |

### Verse 20
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "The first of your dough, a cake, you shall raise as a contribution; as the contribution of a threshing floor, thus you shall raise it" |
| **LOGOS** | "Of the first of your dough you shall present a cake as a contribution; as the contribution of the threshing floor, so shall you present it" |
| **PNEUMA** | "From the first of your dough you shall set aside a loaf as a contribution -- just as you set aside the contribution from the threshing floor, so shall you set this aside" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA follows LOGOS: "Of the first of your dough you shall present a cake as a contribution; as the contribution of the threshing floor, so shall you present it." PNEUMA uses "loaf" for *khallah* (better rendered "cake") and "set aside" for *tarimu*. EMET's word order is too literal. |

### Verse 22
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "And when you err and not do all these commandments the these which YHWH spoke unto Moses" |
| **LOGOS** | "And when you err and do not observe all these commandments which YHWH has spoken to Moses" |
| **PNEUMA** | "And when you go astray and fail to observe all these commandments that YHWH has spoken to Moses" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA follows LOGOS: "And when you err and do not observe all these commandments which YHWH has spoken to Moses." PNEUMA's "go astray" and "fail to observe" are dynamic glosses. EMET's phrasing is ungrammatical in English. |

### Verse 23
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "all which YHWH commanded unto you by the hand of Moses, from the day which YHWH commanded and onward for your generations" |
| **LOGOS** | "all that YHWH has commanded you by the hand of Moses, from the day that YHWH commanded and onward throughout your generations" |
| **PNEUMA** | "everything that YHWH has commanded you through Moses, from the day YHWH first gave commandment and onward throughout your generations" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA: "all that YHWH has commanded you by the hand of Moses, from the day that YHWH gave commandment and onward throughout your generations." PNEUMA's "through Moses" loses the Hebrew idiom *be-yad* ("by the hand of"). PNEUMA adds "first" which is not in the Hebrew. SOPHIA takes "gave commandment" from PNEUMA as slightly more readable than repeating "commanded." |

### Verse 24
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "and it shall be, if from the eyes of the congregation it was done in error, then shall make all the congregation one young bull as a burnt-offering for a pleasing aroma to YHWH, and its grain-offering and its drink-offering according to the ordinance, and one male goat as a sin-offering" |
| **LOGOS** | "then it shall be, if it was done unintentionally, away from the eyes of the congregation, that the whole congregation shall offer one bull of the herd as a burnt offering, for a pleasing aroma to YHWH, with its grain offering and its drink offering according to the ordinance, and one male goat as a sin offering" |
| **PNEUMA** | "if it was done unintentionally, out of the awareness of the community, then the whole community shall offer one young bull as a burnt offering, as a soothing aroma to YHWH, along with its grain offering and its drink offering according to the established rule, and one male goat as a sin offering" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA: "then it shall be, if it was done unintentionally, away from the eyes of the congregation, that the whole congregation shall offer one young bull as a burnt offering, for a pleasing aroma to YHWH, with its grain offering and its drink offering according to the ordinance, and one male goat as a sin offering." PNEUMA's "community" and "out of the awareness" and "established rule" are dynamic. LOGOS's "bull of the herd" adds detail not strictly needed. |

### Verse 25
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "And shall make atonement the priest upon all the congregation of the Sons of Israel, and it shall be forgiven to them; for an error it is, and they brought their offering, a fire-offering to YHWH, and their sin-offering before YHWH, upon their error" |
| **LOGOS** | "And the priest shall make atonement for the whole congregation of the Sons of Israel, and it shall be forgiven them, for it was an error. And they shall bring their offering, an offering by fire to YHWH, and their sin offering before YHWH, for their error" |
| **PNEUMA** | "And the priest shall make atonement for the whole community of the Sons of Israel, and they shall be forgiven, for it was unintentional. And they shall bring their offering -- a fire offering to YHWH -- along with their sin offering before YHWH for their unintentional error" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA follows LOGOS: "And the priest shall make atonement for the whole congregation of the Sons of Israel, and it shall be forgiven them, for it was an error. And they shall bring their offering, a fire offering to YHWH, and their sin offering before YHWH, for their error." PNEUMA uses "community" and "unintentional" rather than "error" for *shegagah*. EMET's word order is too literal. |

### Verse 26
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "And it shall be forgiven to all the congregation of the Sons of Israel and to the sojourner the sojourning in their midst; for to all the people it was in error" |
| **LOGOS** | "And it shall be forgiven the whole congregation of the Sons of Israel and the sojourner who sojourns among them, for with respect to the whole people it was done in error" |
| **PNEUMA** | "And the whole community of the Sons of Israel shall be forgiven, as well as the foreigner who resides among them, for the entire people acted unintentionally" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA follows LOGOS: "And it shall be forgiven the whole congregation of the Sons of Israel and the sojourner who sojourns among them, for with respect to the whole people it was done in error." PNEUMA restructures the sentence and uses "foreigner" and "unintentionally." |

### Verse 27
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "And if one soul sins in error, then she shall bring near a she-goat, a daughter of her year, as a sin-offering" |
| **LOGOS** | "And if one person sins unintentionally, he shall present a female goat in its first year as a sin offering" |
| **PNEUMA** | "And if a single person sins unintentionally, that person shall present a female goat in its first year as a sin offering" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA: "And if one person sins unintentionally, that person shall present a female goat in its first year as a sin offering." EMET's "soul," "she-goat, daughter of her year" and feminine pronouns are overly literal. LOGOS and PNEUMA agree closely; SOPHIA takes "that person" from PNEUMA for gender-neutral clarity while keeping LOGOS's opening. |

### Verse 28
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "And shall make atonement the priest upon the erring soul, in her sinning in error before YHWH, to make atonement upon her; and it shall be forgiven to him" |
| **LOGOS** | "And the priest shall make atonement before YHWH for the person who erred, when he sins unintentionally, to make atonement for him, and it shall be forgiven him" |
| **PNEUMA** | "And the priest shall make atonement before YHWH for the person who went astray by sinning unintentionally, making atonement on their behalf, and they shall be forgiven" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA follows LOGOS: "And the priest shall make atonement before YHWH for the person who erred, when he sins unintentionally, to make atonement for him, and it shall be forgiven him." EMET's "erring soul" and feminine pronouns are too literal. PNEUMA's "went astray" and plural "they" diverge. |

### Verse 29
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "The native-born among the Sons of Israel and the sojourner the sojourning in their midst -- one law shall be for you, for the one who does in error" |
| **LOGOS** | "For the native among the Sons of Israel and for the sojourner who sojourns among them -- one law shall be for you, for the one who acts unintentionally" |
| **PNEUMA** | "For the native-born among the Sons of Israel and for the foreigner who resides among them -- one law shall apply to you all regarding the one who acts unintentionally" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA: "For the native-born among the Sons of Israel and for the sojourner who sojourns among them -- one law shall be for you, for the one who acts unintentionally." "Native-born" preferred (EMET/PNEUMA). "Sojourner who sojourns" preserved. PNEUMA's "apply to you all regarding" is dynamic. |

### Verse 30
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "And the soul which does with a high hand, from the native-born and from the sojourner -- YHWH he is blaspheming; and shall be cut off the soul the that from the midst of her people" |
| **LOGOS** | "But the person who acts with a high hand, whether native or sojourner, he blasphemes YHWH, and that person shall be cut off from the midst of his people" |
| **PNEUMA** | "But the person who acts defiantly, whether native-born or foreigner -- that one blasphemes YHWH, and that person shall be cut off from among their people" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA: "But the person who acts with a high hand, whether native-born or sojourner -- that one blasphemes YHWH, and that person shall be cut off from the midst of his people." The idiom "with a high hand" (*be-yad ramah*) is preserved (EMET/LOGOS) rather than PNEUMA's "defiantly." "Native-born" preferred. "Sojourner" preferred over "foreigner." |

### Verse 31
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "For the word of YHWH he despised and his commandment he broke; being cut off, she shall be cut off, the soul the that -- her iniquity is in her" |
| **LOGOS** | "For the word of YHWH he has despised, and his commandment he has broken. That person shall surely be cut off; his iniquity is upon him" |
| **PNEUMA** | "Because they have despised the word of YHWH and broken his commandment, that person shall be utterly cut off -- their guilt remains upon them" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA follows LOGOS: "For the word of YHWH he has despised, and his commandment he has broken. That person shall surely be cut off; his iniquity is upon him." EMET preserves the infinitive absolute with feminine gender ("being cut off, she shall be cut off") -- this is technically faithful but the convention requires English doubling, and "shall surely be cut off" achieves that. PNEUMA's "utterly cut off" is acceptable but "surely" is the more standard infinitive absolute rendering. PNEUMA's "guilt remains upon them" paraphrases *avonah bah*. |

### Verse 34
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "And they placed him in the guardhouse, for it had not been declared what should be done to him" |
| **LOGOS** | "And they placed him in custody, for it had not been declared what should be done to him" |
| **PNEUMA** | "And they placed him under guard, because it had not yet been declared what should be done to him" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA follows LOGOS: "And they placed him in custody, for it had not been declared what should be done to him." EMET's "guardhouse" is a specific rendering of *mishmar*; "custody" is more standard. PNEUMA adds "yet" which is not in the Hebrew. |

### Verse 35
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "And said YHWH unto Moses: 'Dying, the man shall die; stoning, all the congregation shall stone him with stones outside the camp'" |
| **LOGOS** | "And YHWH said to Moses, 'The man shall surely be put to death. The whole congregation shall stone him with stones outside the camp'" |
| **PNEUMA** | "And YHWH said to Moses, 'The man must surely be put to death. The whole community shall stone him with stones outside the camp'" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA follows LOGOS: "And YHWH said to Moses, 'The man shall surely be put to death. The whole congregation shall stone him with stones outside the camp.'" EMET's "Dying, the man shall die" preserves the infinitive absolute more literally but "shall surely be put to death" is the standard English doubling convention. PNEUMA's "must surely" and "community" diverge slightly. |

### Verse 38
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "Speak unto the Sons of Israel, and say unto them, and they shall make for themselves a tassel upon the corners of their garments for their generations; and they shall put upon the tassel of the corner a thread of blue" |
| **LOGOS** | "Speak to the Sons of Israel and say to them that they shall make for themselves tassels on the corners of their garments throughout their generations, and they shall put on the tassel of each corner a cord of blue" |
| **PNEUMA** | "Speak to the Sons of Israel and tell them to make for themselves tassels on the corners of their garments throughout their generations, and they shall put a cord of blue-violet on the tassel of each corner" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA follows LOGOS: "Speak to the Sons of Israel and say to them that they shall make for themselves tassels on the corners of their garments throughout their generations, and they shall put on the tassel of each corner a cord of blue." EMET uses singular "tassel" and "thread" vs. LOGOS/PNEUMA "tassels" and "cord." PNEUMA uses "blue-violet" for *tekhelet* and "tell them to make" rather than "say to them that they shall make." "Cord" is preferred for *petil* over "thread." "Blue" is the standard rendering of *tekhelet*; "blue-violet" is an interpretive enhancement. |

### Verse 39
| Translator | Rendering |
|---|---|
| **EMET** | "And it shall be for you as a tassel, and you shall see it and you shall remember all the commandments of YHWH, and you shall do them; and not shall you spy out after your heart and after your eyes which you whoring after them" |
| **LOGOS** | "And it shall be a tassel for you, and you shall see it and remember all the commandments of YHWH and do them, and you shall not seek after your own heart and your own eyes, after which you go whoring" |
| **PNEUMA** | "And it shall serve as a tassel for you, and when you look at it, you will remember all the commandments of YHWH and do them, so that you do not go exploring after your own heart and after your own eyes, which you are prone to chase after unfaithfully" |
| **Resolution** | SOPHIA: "And it shall be a tassel for you, and you shall see it and remember all the commandments of YHWH and do them, and you shall not go seeking after your own heart and after your own eyes, after which you go whoring." EMET's "spy out" is the most literal for *taturu* but is awkward. LOGOS's "seek" is too generic. PNEUMA's "go exploring...chase after unfaithfully" is a dynamic expansion. SOPHIA uses "go seeking" as a compromise that preserves the active verb of pursuit. "Whoring" is preserved from EMET/LOGOS for *zonim*. |

---

*-- SOPHIA, AI Translator and Arbiter*
